_**Agent (model claude-4.5-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is showing me a screenshot where the dropdown menu for adding a new message (User message, Agent message) is getting hidden below the main dialog box. Looking at the code, I need to find where this dropdown is rendered and fix its z-index or positioning.

Looking at the code around line 1039-1122, I can see the dropdown menu for adding messages:

```jsx
{addMessageDropdownOpen && (
  <>
    <div
      className="fixed inset-0 z-[150]"
      onClick={() => setAddMessageDropdownOpen(false)}
    />
    <div
      className={`absolute bg-[#2a2a2a] rounded-lg shadow-xl z-[200] overflow-hidden py-1 whitespace-nowrap ${
        message.role === "user"
          ? "right-0 bottom-full mb-2"
          : "left-0 top-10"
      }`}
    >
```

The dropdown has `z-[200]` which should be high enough. But looking at the parent container:

```jsx
<div className="w-2/3 flex flex-col bg-[#0a0a0a] overflow-visible">
  {/* Chat Messages Area */}
  <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto overflow-x-visible p-6">
```

And the outer dialog:
```jsx
<div className="relative w-full max-w-7xl h-[85vh] bg-black rounded-2xl shadow-2xl flex overflow-hidden">
```

The main dialog has `overflow-hidden` which could be clipping the dropdown. Let me look at the dropdown positioning more carefully.

The dropdown is positioned absolutely relative to its parent button:
```jsx
<div className="relative">
  <button
    onClick={() =>
      setAddMessageDropdownOpen(!addMessageDropdownOpen)
    }
```

The issue is that the dropdown is being clipped by one of the parent containers with `overflow-hidden`.


The dropdown appears below the action buttons and gets cut off at the dialog boundary. I have a few options: remove the `overflow-hidden` constraint (though this risks breaking the layout), render the dropdown as a portal outside the overflow container, or reposition it to open upward for agent messages like it does for user messages. For user messages it already opens upward with `bottom-full mb-2`, but agent messages open downward with `top-10`, which is where the clipping happens.

The parent dialog container has `overflow-hidden` applied, which clips anything extending beyond its bounds. Since the user wants to preserve the current appearance for other elements, I need to target just the dropdown positioning—either by using a portal approach or by changing the dropdown direction for agent/tool_call messages to match the user message behavior. 1. Switch the dropdown to `fixed` positioning and calculate coordinates from the button's location
2. Or consistently open dropdowns upward (using `bottom-full`) regardless of message type

Looking at the current implementation, user messages open upward with `right-0 bottom-full mb-2`, while agent and tool_call messages open downward with `left-0 top-10`. The downward-opening dropdown gets clipped when the message is near the bottom of the scrollable area. Opening all dropdowns upward would solve this, though I need to verify it doesn't change the visual appearance for different message types. The dropdown shows options like "User message" and "Agent message", and appears to be cut off in the current view.

The positioning logic at lines 1039-1122 uses absolute positioning with role-based classes. For agent messages, `top-10` causes the downward opening that leads to clipping. Switching to `bottom-full mb-2` for all message types would ensure the dropdown always appears above the button, preventing overflow issues while keeping changes minimal.</details></think>

---

Looking at the code, the issue is that the dropdown for adding messages opens downward for agent messages (`top-10`) which causes it to get clipped by the parent dialog's `overflow-hidden`. 

The fix is to make the dropdown open upward (`bottom-full mb-2`) for agent/tool_call messages as well, similar to how it already works for user messages.

I need to make the dropdown open downward for messages at the top (or first messages) and upward for messages at the bottom. One simple approach is to check if it's the first message and open downward, otherwise open upward.

Let me look at the code structure again. The dropdown is rendered inside the map loop over `chatMessages`, and we have access to `index` and `chatMessages.length`. 

Currently the logic is:
- For user messages: `right-0 bottom-full mb-2` (opens upward, right-aligned)
- For agent/tool_call: `left-0 bottom-full mb-2` (opens upward, left-aligned)

I should change it to:
- If it's near the top of the list (e.g., first message or index is small), open downward
- If it's near the bottom, open upward

Since this dropdown only appears on the last message (`index === chatMessages.length - 1`), I need to think about this differently. If there's only one message (the first and last), it should open downward. If there are multiple messages, the last one should open upward.

Actually a simpler approach: if the total number of messages is small (like 1-2 messages), open downward. Otherwise open upward.

Or even simpler: check if `chatMessages.length <= 1` or `index === 0` to open downward.

Since the + button only appears on the last message (`index === chatMessages.length - 1`), we can check if `chatMessages.length <= 2` to decide direction - if there are only 1-2 messages, open downward, otherwise open upward.

Let me think about this more carefully:


- If there's only 1 message, the dropdown would be at the top of the chat area, so it should open downward
- If there are many messages, the last message would be towards the bottom, so it should open upward

A reasonable threshold might be: if `chatMessages.length <= 2`, open downward; otherwise open upward. This way, when there are very few messages, the dropdown expands downward to avoid going off-screen above the dialog.</details></think>

```diff
+ "use client";
+ 
+ import { useState } from "react";
+ import { INBUILT_TOOLS, getInbuiltToolIcon } from "@/constants/inbuilt-tools";
+ 
+ export type AvailableTool = {
+   uuid: string;
+   name: string;
+   config: Record&lt;string, any&gt;;
+   created_at: string;
+   updated_at: string;
+ };
+ 
+ type ToolPickerProps = {
+   availableTools: AvailableTool[];
+   isLoading: boolean;
+   onSelectInbuiltTool: (toolId: string, toolName: string) =&gt; void;
+   onSelectCustomTool: (
+     tool: AvailableTool,
+     params: Array&lt;{ name: string; value: string }&gt;
+   ) =&gt; void;
+ };
+ 
+ // Helper function to get tool parameters
+ export const getToolParams = (
+   tool: AvailableTool
+ ): Array&lt;{ name: string; value: string }&gt; =&gt; {
+   const props =
+     tool.config?.parameters?.properties ||
+     tool.config?.function?.parameters?.properties ||
+     tool.config?.properties ||
+     {};
+   return Object.keys(props).map((key) =&gt; ({ name: key, value: "" }));
+ };
+ 
+ export function ToolPicker({
+   availableTools,
+   isLoading,
+   onSelectInbuiltTool,
+   onSelectCustomTool,
+ }: ToolPickerProps) {
+   const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState("");
+ 
+   // Filter tools based on search query
+   const filteredTools = availableTools.filter(
+     (tool) =&gt;
+       tool.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()) ||
+       (tool.config?.description &amp;&amp;
+         tool.config.description.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase()))
+   );
+ 
+   const filteredInbuiltTools = INBUILT_TOOLS.filter((tool) =&gt;
+     tool.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.toLowerCase())
+   );
+ 
+   return (
+     &lt;&gt;
+       {/* Search Input */}
+       &lt;div className="p-3 border-b border-[#333]"&gt;
+         &lt;input
+           type="text"
+           value={searchQuery}
+           onChange={(e) =&gt; setSearchQuery(e.target.value)}
+           placeholder="Search tools"
+           className="w-full h-9 px-3 rounded-lg text-sm bg-black text-white placeholder:text-gray-500 border border-[#2a2a2a] focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-gray-500"
+           autoFocus
+         /&gt;
+       &lt;/div&gt;
+ 
+       {/* Tools List */}
+       &lt;div className="max-h-[250px] overflow-y-auto"&gt;
+         {isLoading ? (
+           &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-center py-6"&gt;
+             &lt;svg
+               className="w-5 h-5 animate-spin text-gray-400"
+               fill="none"
+               viewBox="0 0 24 24"
+             &gt;
+               &lt;circle
+                 className="opacity-25"
+                 cx="12"
+                 cy="12"
+                 r="10"
+                 stroke="currentColor"
+                 strokeWidth="4"
+               &gt;&lt;/circle&gt;
+               &lt;path
+                 className="opacity-75"
+                 fill="currentColor"
+                 d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z"
+               &gt;&lt;/path&gt;
+             &lt;/svg&gt;
+           &lt;/div&gt;
+         ) : (
+           &lt;&gt;
+             {/* In-built tools section */}
+             {filteredInbuiltTools.length &gt; 0 &amp;&amp; (
+               &lt;div className="px-3 py-2 text-xs font-semibold text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider border-b border-[#333]"&gt;
+                 In-built tools
+               &lt;/div&gt;
+             )}
+             {filteredInbuiltTools.map((tool) =&gt; (
+               &lt;button
+                 key={tool.id}
+                 onClick={() =&gt; onSelectInbuiltTool(tool.id, tool.name)}
+                 className="w-full px-3 py-2.5 flex items-center gap-3 text-white hover:bg-[#2a2a2a] transition-colors cursor-pointer border-b border-[#333] last:border-b-0"
+               &gt;
+                 &lt;svg
+                   className="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400"
+                   fill="none"
+                   viewBox="0 0 24 24"
+                   stroke="currentColor"
+                   strokeWidth={1.5}
+                 &gt;
+                   &lt;path
+                     strokeLinecap="round"
+                     strokeLinejoin="round"
+                     d={getInbuiltToolIcon(tool.icon)}
+                   /&gt;
+                 &lt;/svg&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="text-left"&gt;
+                   &lt;p className="text-sm font-medium"&gt;{tool.name}&lt;/p&gt;
+                   &lt;p className="text-xs text-gray-500"&gt;Built-in tool&lt;/p&gt;
+                 &lt;/div&gt;
+               &lt;/button&gt;
+             ))}
+ 
+             {/* Custom tools section */}
+             {filteredTools.length &gt; 0 &amp;&amp; (

+                 User defined tools

+             {filteredTools.map((tool) =&gt; {

+                     /&gt;
+                   &lt;/svg&gt;
+                   &lt;div className="text-left"&gt;
+                     &lt;p className="text-sm font-medium"&gt;{tool.name}&lt;/p&gt;
+                     &lt;p className="text-xs text-gray-500"&gt;
+                       {tool.config?.description
+                         ? tool.config.description.length &gt; 40
+                           ? tool.config.description.substring(0, 40) + "..."
+                           : tool.config.description
+                         : params.length &gt; 0
+                         ? &#96;${params.length} parameter${params.length &gt; 1 ? "s" : ""}&#96;
+                         : "No parameters"}
+                     &lt;/p&gt;
+                   &lt;/div&gt;
+                 &lt;/button&gt;
+               );
+             })}
+ 
+             {/* Empty state */}
+             {filteredTools.length === 0 &amp;&amp; filteredInbuiltTools.length === 0 &amp;&amp; (
+               &lt;div className="py-6 text-center text-gray-400 text-sm"&gt;
+                 {searchQuery ? "No tools match your search" : "No tools available"}
+               &lt;/div&gt;
+             )}
+           &lt;/&gt;
+         )}
+       &lt;/div&gt;
+     &lt;/&gt;
+   );
+ }
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>
